formulas.name: Economic Activities
formulas.formula: Primary activities are the first point of contact between humans and the natural environment.
formulas.notes: These activities involve the direct extraction of natural resources from the environment.

formulas.name: Subsistence Activities
formulas.formula: Subsistence activities are those that provide for the immediate needs of individuals or families.
formulas.notes: These activities are essential for the survival of people in rural or isolated areas.

formulas.name: Extraction of Natural Resources
formulas.formula: Extraction of natural resources involves the removal of raw materials from the earth.
formulas.notes: This can include activities such as mining, logging, and fishing.

formulas.name: Primary Production
formulas.formula: Primary production refers to the direct extraction of natural resources from the environment.
formulas.notes: This can include agriculture, animal husbandry, and fishing.

formulas.name: Types of Primary Activities
formulas.formula: Primary activities can be classified into subsistence and commercial activities.
formulas.notes: Subsistence activities are those that provide for the immediate needs of individuals or families, while commercial activities are those that generate income.

formulas.name: Role of Primary Activities in Economic Development
formulas.formula: Primary activities play a crucial role in economic development by providing raw materials for secondary and tertiary activities.
formulas.notes: These activities also generate income and employment for people in rural and urban areas.
